entries.value: Basis Package Manager is open source under the MIT License. It's built with these third-party projects — thank you to their authors.
It looks like the app closed unexpectedly last time. Open a pre-filled GitHub issue so we can look into it?
Add a desktop shortcut for Basis Package Manager? You can always find it from the Start menu.
Couldn't find bundle “{0}” in the registry.
Clone or add a Basis install first, then install {0}.
Install link for {0} was missing its git URL.
Open a pre-filled GitHub issue with this error and your last actions
Running from source — updates are handled by your build, not the in-app updater.
Unity {0} isn't installed. Add it on the Unity Editors tab, then try again.
Unity {0} isn't installed — opened Unity Hub so you can add it.
Installed version — check for updates in Settings
Update Core runs 'git pull', which can change files in your Basis project.

{0}

Back up {1} first? This zips Assets, Packages and ProjectSettings (not the Library cache) into a BasisBackups folder next to your install.

Only {0} is free on {1}. Cloning Basis and opening it in Unity can need well over that, and the clone may fail partway.

Clone here anyway?
Add all {0} recommended Basis packages to this project now? You can manage them anytime on the Packages tab.
Use Clone Basis above to pull down BasisVR/Basis, or Add existing to register a clone you already have.
Clone the BasisVR/Basis repository, then manage each project's packages, updates and local changes.
Choose a folder to clone Basis into
Very low disk space — only {0} free on {1}. Cloning Basis will likely fail; free up space or choose another drive.
Low disk space — {0} free on {1}. Basis plus its Unity Library can use 20 GB or more.
That install is already in the list.
Choose a folder to clone into first.
Clone cancelled — low disk space on the target drive.
{0} already exists and is not empty.
Git was not found. Install Git and add it to your PATH (see Settings).
{0} has no Unity project to back up.
Removed {0} from the list (files left on disk).
Requires Unity {0} — open the Unity tab to install it.
Zip Assets, Packages and ProjectSettings (not the Library cache)
Cloned into Packages/ for editing — it uses the local folder, not the registry git URL. Manage it on the Develop tab; Swap back restores the git version.
Paste a GitHub URL, or owner/repo[?path=Sub/Folder][#branch]. Its package.json is added to your Unity manifest as a git dependency.
Clone or select a Basis install on the Installs tab to manage its packages.
No install with a Unity project
Pick a project, then install packages from the Basis registry or add any community UPM package by git URL.
That bundle file has no packages.
That file isn’t a valid bundle: {0}
Couldn't parse the package's git URL.
Install link was missing the package id or git URL.
This project has no add-on packages to bundle yet.
{0} isn't a git package — no versions to choose.
Open an install with a Unity project first, then click Install for {0} again.
Opening a GitHub issue to submit “{0}” ({1} packages)…
Could not load package.json from {0}/{1}{2} — check the path and that the repo is public.
Paste a GitHub URL or owner/repo.
No packages available to install — check your connection, then try Refresh on the Packages tab.
Refused to add {0}: the install link's git URL uses an unsupported or unsafe transport.
({0} skipped for an unsafe git URL)
Pick a specific release, beta, or the default branch
Filter installed packages by owner (from the package id)
Switch between list and grid layout
No Unity editors installed yet — add one below.
Detect installed Unity Editors and install the version Basis requires.
Pick a Unity 6 release. The changeset is filled in automatically from Unity's release feed.
Install kicked off via Unity Hub for {0}.
Pick a Unity 6 release from the dropdown first.
Your Basis install targets Unity {0}. Install that exact version below for a clean open.
GitHub CLI found but not logged in — run “gh auth login”, then Re-check.
Not signed in — paste a token below, or install & log in with the GitHub CLI.
Unity keeps git packages in Library/PackageCache. If one was edited there by accident, scan to detect it and open a pull request upstream.
No local changes found in cached packages.
Scanning cached packages for local changes…
No local changes in cached packages.
Found local changes in {0} cached package(s).

{0} is already mounted at Packages/{0}.

Replace it with a fresh clone? Any local edits in that folder will be lost.

Packages/{0} already exists and isn't empty — it may be a leftover mount or a local copy.

Replace it with a fresh clone? Its current contents will be deleted.
Select a Basis install on the Installs tab to edit its packages.
No install with a Unity project
Mounting a package and submitting pull requests need Git. Install it, then Re-check.
Mount a git package to edit it in Unity, then open a pull request upstream — swap back or keep it when you're done.
No git-loaded packages in this project.
Git is required to mount a package. Install it, then Re-check.
No changes to submit for {0} — edit it in Unity first.
Mounted {0} into Packages/ — edit it in Unity, then Submit PR.
Sign in to GitHub first (GitHub CLI or a token).
{0} is back on its git URL.
Swapping {0} back to its git URL…
That token didn't authenticate — make sure it has the “repo” scope.
Pick an install on the Installs tab to see what you have changed.
This install is not a git repository, so local changes can't be tracked.
What you have changed in the Basis source since your last commit.
Basis Package Manager updates itself from GitHub Releases. When a new version is published an update banner appears at the top of the window — one click downloads it and restarts.
JSON URL serving a Basis-compatible catalog. Leave blank to use the Basis package registry (basisvr.org/packages).

Add package catalogs from other sources. These are UNOFFICIAL — not reviewed or vetted by BasisVR — so install from them at your own risk. Official Basis packages always take priority over an unofficial one with the same id.
Where new Basis clones are created by default.
Git not found on PATH — install it from git-scm.com.
Clone location, catalog source and tooling paths.
Choose the language for the Basis Package Manager interface. Applies immediately.
What the app did this session — personal paths, usernames and IPs are redacted. Attach these when reporting a problem.
Erase all Basis Package Manager settings and data on this computer and return it to a fresh, first-time state. Your actual Basis clones and files on disk are not touched.

This permanently erases all your settings and data — your project list, catalog URLs, preferences, language, activity logs and mounted-package records — and returns Basis Package Manager to a fresh, first-time state. Your Basis clones and files on disk are NOT deleted. This can't be undone.
Everything was reset — this is a fresh start.
Mount git packages locally, edit them in Unity, and open pull requests. Hidden for non-developers.
A git status + per-file diff of your install. Hidden by default.
Receive prerelease updates (experimental — may be broken)
Get early builds as soon as they're published — frequent and sometimes unstable. Off = stable releases only. Applies on Save.
Path to the Unity Hub binary, if not auto-detected.
Individuals back Basis on OpenCollective — monthly or one-time. These funds primarily support full-time maintenance and developer-experience work.

•  Monthly & one-time options
•  Public backer wall & transparent ledger
Companies sponsor roadmap items or co-fund features — networking, avatar systems, tooling. Work lands upstream and stays MIT-licensed by default.

•  Shared infrastructure over bespoke forks
•  Clear deliverables, open development
Basis is MIT-licensed and creator-first. Community donations and company collaborations keep a maintainer working full-time and speed up releases.

Funds are pooled to tackle shared problems — networking, embodiment, tooling — that benefit everyone. There's a public ledger on OpenCollective, and sponsored work is attributed in release notes. Sponsors don't control the project.
Stuck on something the docs don't cover? The Discord is the fastest way to get help from other creators and the maintainers.
Guides, API reference and how-tos for building on Basis — plus the community to ask when you're stuck.
Installation, first steps, avatars, worlds, networking and the SDK — the full docs live at docs.basisvr.org.
Read the code, file a bug or open a pull request. Basis is MIT-licensed and developed in the open.
What the app did this session — personal paths, usernames and IPs are redacted. Attach these when reporting a problem.
Check back later — news from the Basis team will show up here.
Updates and news from the Basis team.
A friendly name shown across the app for this clone.
“Remove from list” just forgets this project here — your files stay exactly where they are. “Delete from disk” permanently deletes the whole project folder shown below. This cannot be undone.
Pick at least one package to include.
Bundle your Basis + packages. Save it to a file to keep or share locally, or submit it to the public registry.
Choose which install to add this package to.
Everything above, plus the Develop tab — mount packages locally, edit them in Unity, and open pull requests.
How will you use Basis? We'll tailor which tools are shown — you can change this anytime in Settings.
Clone Basis, install packages, and keep it up to date. The essentials, nothing extra.
Contribute your changes to {0} back to its repository.
What did you change and why?
We create this branch, commit your changes, push (forking first if you can't push directly), and open the PR.
Pick a published release, or the latest default branch.
No releases published — showing tags / the default branch. Ask the creator to cut releases for stable versions.
What's new with Basis, where to learn it, and how to help keep it going.
Check back later — news from the Basis team will show up here.
Installation, first steps, avatars, worlds, networking and the SDK — the full docs live at docs.basisvr.org.
Stuck on something the docs don't cover? The Discord is the fastest way to get help from other creators and the maintainers.
Read the code, file a bug or open a pull request. Basis is MIT-licensed and developed in the open.
Basis is MIT-licensed and creator-first. Community donations and company collaborations keep a maintainer working full-time and speed up releases.
Individuals back Basis on OpenCollective — monthly or one-time. These funds primarily support full-time maintenance and developer-experience work.
Companies sponsor roadmap items or co-fund features — networking, avatar systems, tooling. Work lands upstream and stays MIT-licensed by default.

Funds are pooled to tackle shared problems — networking, embodiment, tooling — that benefit everyone. There's a public ledger on OpenCollective, and sponsored work is attributed in release notes. Sponsors don't control the project.
